accepted_warnings.operation: first /ready request after restart or cold observability refresh
accepted_warnings.environment: local Debian installed stable runtime on the release reference host
accepted_warnings.user_impact: The first readiness check after restart can be visibly slower, but steady-state /ready is sub-10 ms in repeated warm samples.
accepted_warnings.reason_not_fixed: The cold path builds bounded runtime observability once. Removing it would make startup truth less useful, and it does not affect steady-state readiness polling.
mitigations: Detailed observability remains outside the readiness success decision.
prove_ready.py reports accepted latency warnings separately from unresolved warnings.
accepted_warnings.revisit_trigger: warm /ready p95 exceeds 250 ms or cold /ready repeatedly exceeds 2500 ms on the reference host

accepted_warnings.operation: uncached /search/status or search-status chat when managed SearXNG must be probed
accepted_warnings.environment: local Debian installed stable runtime on the release reference host
accepted_warnings.user_impact: The first search-health miss can take roughly one loopback probe timeout. Cache hits are effectively immediate.
accepted_warnings.reason_not_fixed: The probe is bounded and truthful; declaring search healthy without the probe would be incorrect.
mitigations: A short bounded cache is used for repeated status checks.
Search unavailability is reported as degraded/unavailable, not healthy.
accepted_warnings.revisit_trigger: cache-hit p95 exceeds 150 ms or uncached probe repeatedly exceeds 2500 ms

accepted_warnings.operation: package install preview and pending confirmation lookup for htop
accepted_warnings.environment: local Debian installed stable runtime on the release reference host
accepted_warnings.sample_count: measured by scripts/runtime_latency_investigation.py, rc1_latency_closure_smoke.py, and perf_smoke.py
accepted_warnings.dominant_span: full /chat round trip under local runtime noise; direct dpkg-query lookup remains below budget
accepted_warnings.user_impact: The Plan preview may take a few seconds in noisy installed-product runs, but it does not mutate and remains authorization-correct.
accepted_warnings.reason_not_fixed: The direct package-state check is cheap; remaining cost is the full chat route and local runtime contention. Correctness requires preserving Plan creation, target binding, and confirmation state.
mitigations: Redundant pending-item read during follow-up resolution was removed.
perf_smoke now uses robust distributions instead of one noisy sample.
Package preview performs no apt update, no network package-manager call, and no install.
accepted_warnings.revisit_trigger: direct package-state p95 exceeds 250 ms, package preview p95 exceeds 3500 ms, or confirmation lookup p95 exceeds 4500 ms

accepted_warnings.operation: read-only runtime, Telegram, and search status prompts through /chat
accepted_warnings.environment: local Debian installed stable runtime on the release reference host
accepted_warnings.dominant_span: full local /chat round trip; server-side route spans are usually lower than the client-observed wall time
accepted_warnings.user_impact: Some read-only status prompts can take a few seconds on the local installed service under load, but they do not mutate and still return truthful status.
accepted_warnings.reason_not_fixed: Latest evidence points to installed runtime round-trip noise and bounded status probes rather than an authorization or store-scan defect. Removing status truth checks would make answers less accurate.
mitigations: perf_smoke now reports distributions and server timing metadata.
runtime_latency_investigation.py measures these status chats explicitly.
accepted_warnings.revisit_trigger: runtime, Telegram, or search status chat p95 exceeds 4500 ms on the reference host, or server-side orchestrator spans repeatedly exceed 1500 ms
